raw.objective: “to what degree patient characteristics (age, body weight, dose and gender) influence pharmacokinetics of the drug”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, AGE, DDVPA, SEX, comedication (CZP) Included: WT, CZP, SEX, DDVPA
raw.observations: CPZ and VPA interaction causes CPZ CL to increase and VPA CL to decrease, in comparison to monotherapy. WT causes a decrease in clearance Retrospective data used; large residual variability may originate from variation in sampling time, and true day-today variability in drug disposition. Effects on F and CL cannot be separated in model as CL/F is estimated.
raw.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
raw.equations: CL = (((theta1*(WT**theta2)*(DMGKG**theta3)*(theta4**CLNZ)* (theta5**(1-SEX)))*WT)/1000)*(1+eta1) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) DMGKG: VPA dose (mg/kg/day) WT: Total body weight (kg) CLNZ: comedication clonazepam (1:yes, 0:no) SEX: gender (0:male, 1:female)
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 17.2 theta2= -0.264 theta3= 0.159 theta4= 0.821 theta5 = 0.896 omega1= 0.0185 sigma1= 0.0355

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL = (((theta1*(WT**theta2)*(DMGKG**theta3)*(theta4**CLNZ)* (theta5**(1-SEX)))*WT)/1000)*(1+eta1) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) DMGKG: VPA dose (mg/kg/day) WT: Total body weight (kg) CLNZ: comedication clonazepam (1:yes, 0:no) SEX: gender (0:male, 1:female)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Steady state, oral doses in patients
study.objective: “to what degree patient characteristics (age, body weight, dose and gender) influence pharmacokinetics of the drug”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, AGE, DDVPA, SEX, comedication (CZP) Included: WT, CZP, SEX, DDVPA

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
